aliases: 班兰椰浆糕 (Bān Lán Yē Jiāng Gāo)

chinese: 班兰椰浆糕 (Bān Lán Yē Jiāng Gāo)

food_subgroup: Traditional steamed kueh — two-layer glutinous rice base with pandan coconut custard top

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Kueh Salat / Seri Muka. No sufficiently specific FoodOn class for Peranakan two-layer steamed glutinous rice kueh with pandan custard topping.
ontology.foodex2_description: Traditional layered steamed cake — closest parent match in FoodEx2; no exact code for Kueh Salat exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0GBQ used as closest defensible parent. No exact FoodEx2 entry for Peranakan glutinous rice kueh.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for Kueh Salat or equivalent traditional Peranakan kueh.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of Kueh Salat as sold at Bengawan Solo or hawker markets weighs approximately 90g and provides around 225 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Kueh Salat — Health Promotion Board Singapore Food Composition Database and Bengawan Solo product label
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced from HPB Singapore food composition data for traditional steamed glutinous rice kueh with coconut milk and pandan custard, cross-referenced with Bengawan Solo commercial product label. Minor micronutrient values (copper, manganese, vitamin K) estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for glutinous rice and coconut milk where HPB does not report these fields. All values represent per-100g basis for a standard hawker/commercial preparation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of glutinous rice and analogous steamed glutinous rice preparations — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 70 assigned based on University of Sydney GI Database values for glutinous (waxy) rice, which is amylopectin-dominant and consistently yields GI 70–98 in published studies. Kueh Salat bottom layer is steamed glutinous rice in coconut milk; the pandan custard top adds sugar but coconut milk fat provides partial GI moderation. GL per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (70 × 32.0) / 100 = 22.4, using 90g portion providing ~32g available carbohydrate. Confidence 0.9 — no direct GI measurement for Kueh Salat exists in the University of Sydney database; estimate is based on the glutinous rice base as the dominant glycaemic driver.

hypertension.guidance: Kueh Salat is low in sodium (~80mg per piece) and poses no acute blood pressure concern; however, the high saturated fat content (7g per piece from coconut milk) warrants consideration for long-term cardiovascular risk management in hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) and high glycemic load (~22.4 per 90g piece) driven by amylopectin-dominant glutinous rice base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit Kueh Salat to one piece maximum per occasion or avoid it entirely, as the GI of ~70 and GL of ~22.4 per 90g piece is equivalent to the glycaemic impact of approximately 1.4 bowls of white rice; lower-GI kueh alternatives such as Soon Kueh (GI ~55) are preferable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat content (~7g per 90g piece, ~35% of daily limit) from coconut milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each piece of Kueh Salat provides approximately 7g of saturated fat (predominantly lauric and myristic acid from coconut milk), representing about 35% of the recommended daily saturated fat limit; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional treats and monitor overall saturated fat intake across the day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kueh Salat, also known as Seri Muka or Kuih Seri Muka, is a quintessential Peranakan and Malay two-layered steamed kueh widely sold at hawker centres, traditional kueh stalls, and premium retailers such as Bengawan Solo in Singapore and Malaysia. The name 'Seri Muka' means 'beautiful face' in Malay, referring to the smooth, glossy green custard top layer. The bottom layer consists of glutinous (waxy) rice steamed with coconut milk and salt, sometimes tinted blue-purple with blue pea flower (bunga telang). The top layer is a thick, fragrant green custard made from pandan juice, eggs, sugar, and coconut milk, steamed until set to a smooth, firm consistency. The two layers are pressed together and cut into rectangular or diamond-shaped pieces. Texture is a contrast of chewy, dense glutinous rice base and silky, smooth pandan custard. Flavour is a harmonious blend of salty-savoury rice and sweet, fragrant pandan-coconut custard. Per 100g: 250 kcal, 3.3g protein, 8.9g fat (7.0g saturated from coconut milk), 35.6g carbohydrate, 13.3g sugar, 0.6g dietary fibre, 88.9mg sodium. Per standard piece (~90g): ~225 kcal, ~32g carbohydrate, ~7g saturated fat, ~80mg sodium. Glycemic index ~70 (HIGH) due to amylopectin-dominant glutinous rice; glycemic load ~22.4 per piece — clinically significant for Type 2 Diabetes management. Halal, vegetarian, gluten-free, dairy-free. Contains eggs (allergen). Available at Bengawan Solo, Old Chang Kee, and hawker markets across Singapore and Malaysia.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and secondary nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore food composition database and cross-referenced with Bengawan Solo commercial product label, both approved primary sources for Singaporean dishes. GI estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for glutinous rice as the dominant glycaemic driver. Minor micronutrients (copper, manganese, vitamin K) estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data where HPB does not report these fields. Confidence rated high overall given strong primary source alignment for macros and key secondary nutrients.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for traditional steamed glutinous rice kueh with coconut milk is not reported in the HPB Singapore food composition database or Bengawan Solo product label as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this preparation is not reported in HPB Singapore database or Bengawan Solo label. trans_fat_g reported as 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero per HPB Singapore and product label; no hydrogenated fats used in traditional preparation. Copper (0.1mg/100g), manganese (0.3mg/100g), and vitamin K (1.5mcg/100g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for glutinous rice and coconut milk (confidence 0.9); these are not directly reported in HPB Singapore for this dish. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for glutinous/waxy rice; no direct GI measurement for Kueh Salat exists in the database. GL per serving = (70 × 32.0) / 100 = 22.4 for a 90g piece providing ~32g available carbohydrate. All core macros (protein_g 3.3, fat_g 8.9, carbohydrate_g 35.6) are non-zero and nutritionally plausible for this preparation — no zero-macro justification required. FoodEx2 code A0GBQ used as closest defensible parent (traditional layered steamed cake); no exact FoodEx2 code exists for Kueh Salat. Tamil and Hindi local names are phonetic transliterations; no established native-script culinary term for this dish exists in Tamil or Hindi culinary literature.

Confirmed trans_fat_g 0.0 as confirmed zero per source.
